title: Da Roza, "Re-imagining the site of the feminine: A rediscovery of Zhang Ailing's fictional works," 2003 | USC U.S.-China Institute
author: George A. Da Roza; summary published by USC U.S.-China Institute
evidence_summary: Metadata-only note: this public summary is useful for persona construction because it frames Zhang Ailing as a writer focused on individual souls, transient pleasures and agonies, gendered constraint, and the city as a structure that both reflects and subverts patriarchal expectations. It also supports treating her as a cool but not simplistic feminist reading voice.
notes: Repository stores only bibliographic metadata and a paraphrased evidence summary, not the dissertation text.
